title: Rumohra adiantiformis
taxonomy: Rumohra adiantiformis is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida and the division Pteridophyta. The order to which it belongs is Polypodiales. Within this order, it falls under the family Polypodiaceae. This classification places Rumohra adiantiformis among the vascular plants.
uses: Rumohra adiantiformis, also known as the Maidenhair Fern, has been traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help in treating various health issues. The plant is said to have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions. However, there is limited scientific research on the plant's medicinal uses, and more studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness. Further research is also required to understand the potential risks and benefits of using Rumohra adiantiformis for medicinal purposes.
